subject: Computer Networking
title: Symmetric Encryption
description: Learn Symmetric Encryption in one minute.

hook.text: You lock a box with a key. Only someone with the same key can open it. That's symmetric encryption.

buildup.text: AES is today's standard. It scrambles data into unreadable ciphertext using a single shared secret key.

discovery.text: AES is extremely fast. Your laptop encrypts gigabytes per second using hardware acceleration built into the CPU.

twist.text: The problem: how do you share the key securely? If someone intercepts the key, the encryption is worthless.

climax.text: Symmetric encryption pairs with asymmetric for key exchange. Each covers the other's weakness perfectly.

punchline.text: One key locks and unlocks. Fast but sharing it is the hard part.

quiz.question: What is the main challenge of symmetric encryption?
options: Securely sharing the key between parties
The encryption is too slow for modern hardware
It can only encrypt small amounts of data
